meta.description: Gold evaluation set for the Medium Article RAG assistant. article_id == 0-based row index in medium-english-50mb.csv (7682 rows, no rows dropped). Used by the Phase 2 retrieval sweep to compute hit-rate and MRR; no LLM calls required.
meta.id_scheme: 0-based CSV row index, stable across all runs

scoring.single: hit if any id in acceptable_article_ids appears among the deduplicated retrieved article_ids within top-k; reciprocal rank = 1 / rank of first such id.
scoring.multi: hit if at least min_distinct DISTINCT ids from acceptable_article_ids appear among the deduplicated retrieved article_ids within top-k.
scoring.recommendation: hit if any id in acceptable_article_ids appears; primary_article_id is the strongest single match.

questions.question: Find an article that reframes marketing as a conversation with readers, aimed at writers who find self-promotion uncomfortable. Provide the title and author.
questions.evidence: Text: "Marketing is just a conversation between you and your readers. If you can remember that, then you can manage your fear about it." Aimed at introverts (uncomfortable with self-promotion).

questions.question: List exactly 3 articles about education. Return only the titles.
questions.evidence: All 10 acceptable ids have 'education' in the title and are on-topic (education reform, public education, educational AI/chatbots, environmental education, etc.). Must return 3 DISTINCT articles, not 3 chunks of one.

questions.question: Find an article that argues past pandemics (such as the bubonic plague) can spur innovation and recovery, and summarise its central argument.
questions.target_title: Rebounding From The Pandemic… with AI
questions.evidence: Text: "In the mid-1300s, my native Italy was devastated by the bubonic plague... it is also believed to have seeded the Renaissance... one of the most prolific periods of our modern society."

questions.question: I want practical, beginner-friendly advice on building habits that actually stick. Which article would you recommend, and why?
questions.target_title: Building and Breaking Habits — An Essential Guide
questions.evidence: 5977 is an 'Essential Guide' to building/breaking habits with practical steps ('start small'). Acceptable alternates are other practical habit-formation guides (Atomic Habits summaries, 'Magic Key to Making Habits Sticky', 'How To Change A Habit').

questions.question: Find an article explaining how training your sense of smell can strengthen your brain's neural pathways in about six weeks. Give the title and author.
questions.evidence: Subtitle: "How smell training can change your brain in six weeks"; body discusses the olfactory system's plasticity and strengthening neural pathways. (authors field is empty in CSV; author name appears in body text.)

questions.question: Which article breaks down the behavioral-science and psychology principles that Coursera uses to make its online education experience addictive?
questions.target_title: How Coursera uses psychology to make education addictive
questions.evidence: Title and body: "How Coursera uses psychology to make education addictive — The behavioral science principles behind their experience."

questions.question: Summarise the central argument of an article about the decline of trust in experts and where the concept of the 'expert' originated.
questions.target_title: Truth and Trust: the Rise and Fall of the Expert
questions.evidence: Discusses distrust in experts underpinning conspiracy theories and cites William Davies' 'Decline of Reason' on the 17th-century origin of the expert.

questions.question: Summarise the main idea of an article about how the coronavirus pandemic and isolation affect the human brain and mental health.
questions.evidence: Subtitle: "A guide to the curious and troubling impact of the pandemic and isolation"; body links the disease to stroke and worsening mental health.

questions.question: List exactly 3 articles about building or forming good habits. Return only the titles.
questions.evidence: All acceptable ids are habit-formation articles (The Power of Habit, Atomic Habits summaries, 'How To Change A Habit', 'The Magic Key to Making Habits Sticky', 'How I Built a Lasting Exercise Habit', etc.). Must return 3 DISTINCT articles.

questions.question: I'm a creator trying to grow my audience and newsletter subscribers. Which article would you recommend, and why?
questions.target_title: The Ultimate Guide To Growing Your Audience
questions.evidence: 1968 lays out 'the six elements of an effective audience growth strategy' and newsletter growth. 2019 ('12 Very Creative Ways to Promote Your Writing') is an acceptable alternate.
